_comment: Compat matrix per #1027. This file is the spec-driven inventory of every prop / style / API surface Pulp's React + DOM-lite consumers might use, mapped to its current implementation status. Status values: supported | partial | missing | wontfix. The 'mapsTo' field names the bridge / Yoga / Skia path the prop lowers to. The 'tests' field is intentionally empty here and will be filled in by individual fix PRs that ship Catch2 / vitest coverage. The audit was generated 2026-04-30 by walking three reference specs (Yoga via React Native Layout Props, React Native View / Text / Transform Style Props, and the most-used MDN CSS reference) and cross-referencing against four implementation surfaces: packages/pulp-react/src/prop-applier.ts (the @pulp/react switch), core/view/js/web-compat-style-decl.js (the DOM-lite CSS adapter), core/view/src/widget_bridge.cpp (the C++ bridge — 251 register_function entries), and core/view/include/pulp/view/{view,geometry}.hpp (the Flex / View widget surface).

spec_sources.yoga: https://www.yogalayout.dev/docs/styling — list cross-checked against https://reactnative.dev/docs/layout-props which mirrors the upstream Yoga API
spec_sources.css: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties — top ~150 commonly-used properties across layout, box-model, typography, color, transforms, flex/grid, opacity

_audit._notes_on_consumer_surfaces: The same prop can have different status across consumer surfaces. 'css/*' tracks what core/view/js/web-compat-style-decl.js (the DOM-lite el.style adapter) routes; 'rn/*' tracks what packages/pulp-react/src/prop-applier.ts (the React renderer for @pulp/react JSX intrinsics) routes; 'yoga/*' tracks the underlying Yoga-shaped layout surface in core/view/include/pulp/view/geometry.hpp::FlexStyle. Where a prop is supported by one consumer but not another, both are noted explicitly in 'notes'.

batch-labels.batch:logical-properties: CSS / RN / Yoga RTL-adjacent shorthands (margin*Inline*, padding*Block*, start/end, direction)
batch-labels.batch:typography: Label widget plumbing — text-shadow, text-decoration[Line/Style/Color], word-break, line-clamp, fontVariant
batch-labels.batch:transforms: setTransform extension — rotateX/Y/Z, scale[XY], skew[XY], perspective, transformOrigin
batch-labels.batch:effects: Skia paint primitives — outline, mixBlendMode, filter, mask, clipPath, background-image url(), shadow*
batch-labels.batch:layout: Yoga-side or shorthand — alignContent, aspectRatio (real), grid-{areas,auto-*}, flex shorthand, border-* flat props
batch-labels.batch:animation: CSS transition + animation engine — currently stubs
batch-labels.batch:interaction: Already-bridge-registered but not wired through prop-applier — cursor, pointer-events, user-select, touch-action
batch-labels.batch:lists: List intrinsic emulation — list-style*, marker rendering
batch-labels.batch:scroll-augmentation: Augment ScrollView intrinsic — scroll-behavior, scroll-snap, scroll-margin/padding, overscroll-behavior
batch-labels.meta:pseudo-classes: Pseudo-class selector model (:hover, :focus, :active) — orthogonal to property triage

css/display.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'display' -> setVisible(id, false) on 'none', setVisible(id, true) on 'flex'/'block'. 'grid' is acknowledged but only takes effect when gridTemplateColumns/Rows is also set. Yoga has no 'block', 'inline', 'inline-block', 'table', 'contents' modes -- Pulp is flex-only.
css/display.notes: Yoga's underlying view model is exclusively flex. 'block' is silently aliased to 'flex'. RN's display enum is {none, flex, contents} which matches us better than CSS. The Display enum on the C++ View has no direct field -- visibility is implemented via View::set_visible. There is no 'partial' display rendering -- non-flex values render as flex with default direction.

css/position.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'position' -> setPosition. View::Position enum has static_/relative/absolute/fixed/sticky.
css/position.notes: All five CSS values are accepted by the bridge. 'fixed' and 'sticky' may render the same as 'absolute' in practice -- see #779 follow-ups for behavioral verification.

css/top.notes: parseCSSLength is invoked but the bridge stores raw px only -- percentage values are dropped. setTop accepts numbers only.

css/inset.notes: Shorthand expands correctly. Underlying setTop/etc. are px only.

css/width.notes: FlexStyle.preferred_width is a float. Yoga in upstream supports % and auto, but Pulp's C++ port loses that on the JSON wire -- only floats are accepted by setFlex.

css/height.notes: Same as width. dim_height supports DimensionUnit::vw/vh/percent/vmin/vmax internally but the setFlex bridge only accepts a single double.

css/maxWidth.notes: 0 = no maximum (semantic differs from CSS where unspecified = none).

css/aspectRatio.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js parses '16/9' and calls setFlex(id, 'aspect_ratio', ratio); but FlexStyle has NO aspect_ratio field, so setFlex silently drops it
css/aspectRatio.notes: MAJOR GAP -- the parser exists but the layout backend doesn't honor it. The setFlex switch in widget_bridge.cpp:1624-1685 has no 'aspect_ratio' branch.

css/boxSizing.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js calls setBoxSizing if defined -- but the bridge does NOT register setBoxSizing
css/boxSizing.notes: Silent no-op. typeof guard in JS prevents a runtime error but the value is never honored. Yoga's box model is content-box-equivalent.

css/margin.notes: 'margin: auto' centers in CSS -- Pulp does NOT honor auto margins.

css/marginInline.notes: Logical property normalized to physical. No RTL awareness -- always treated as LTR (margin_left = inline-start).

css/marginBlock.notes: Logical property; assumes horizontal writing mode.

css/marginInlineStart.mapsTo: case in switch is bare break (line 609); RTL untracked

css/gap.notes: Two-value 'gap: 10px 20px' is not handled by parseCSSLength -- it parses only the first token.

css/flex.notes: The CSS shorthand keywords (auto/none/initial) parse as NaN and silently set flex_grow=0.

css/flexDirection.notes: MAJOR GAP -- Pulp's FlexDirection enum (geometry.hpp:61) only has {row, column}. Reverse modes silently fall through to default 'col'.

css/flexWrap.notes: FlexStyle.flex_wrap is a bool -- wrap-reverse cannot be expressed.

css/flexBasis.notes: Yoga supports 'auto' and percentages; Pulp accepts only px float.

css/justifyContent.notes: FlexJustify enum covers the 6 RN values.

css/alignItems.notes: FlexAlign enum has {start, center, end, stretch, auto_} -- no baseline support.

css/alignContent.mapsTo: web-compat calls setFlex(id, 'align_content', ...) but setFlex switch has NO 'align_content' branch
css/alignContent.notes: MAJOR GAP -- silently dropped. Multi-line flex layouts have no cross-axis alignment control.

css/placeItems.notes: Misnamed -- second token is mapped to justify_content rather than justify_items (which Yoga doesn't support).

css/placeContent.notes: align_content half is silently dropped (see css/alignContent).

css/overflow.notes: View::Overflow enum has only {visible, hidden}. Scroll requires using the ScrollView intrinsic.

css/visibility.mapsTo: setVisibility(id, 'visible'|'hidden') if registered, else opacity fallback
css/visibility.notes: setVisibility IS registered (widget_bridge.cpp:1814) so the primary path works.

css/backgroundColor.notes: Modern color spaces not supported by parseCSSColor.

css/background.mapsTo: if 'gradient' in value -> setBackgroundGradient; else parseCSSColor -> setBackground
css/background.notes: Background shorthand supports only color OR a gradient.

css/backgroundSize.mapsTo: web-compat calls setBackgroundSize if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/backgroundSize.notes: Silent no-op. Tied to backgroundImage url() which is also missing.

css/backgroundPosition.mapsTo: web-compat calls setBackgroundPosition if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/backgroundRepeat.mapsTo: web-compat calls setBackgroundRepeat if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/border.notes: Border style is parsed but discarded -- only solid is rendered.

css/borderColor.notes: Re-applies border with width=1 and radius=0 -- overwrites any prior radius. KNOWN BUG.

css/borderRadius.notes: Re-applies border with width=0 -- overwrites prior width. KNOWN BUG. Per-corner setters work better.

css/borderTopColor.notes: Sets width to 0 -- must combine with borderTopWidth. Quirk.

css/outline.mapsTo: web-compat calls setOutline if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/outline.notes: Silent no-op. Affects accessibility focus rings.

css/boxShadow.mapsTo: regex parse -> setBoxShadow(id, dx, dy, blur, spread, color, inset)
css/boxShadow.notes: Single-shadow only. Multiple comma-separated shadows are not split.

css/textShadow.mapsTo: web-compat calls setTextShadow if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/fontFamily.notes: FAMOUS BUG -- web-compat-style-decl.js:465 strips quotes from a list and passes the entire 'JetBrains Mono, monospace' as one face name. Should split on comma. Tracked in #932 / #927.

css/fontWeight.notes: Keyword 'bold' parses as NaN -> 400. Should map: normal->400, bold->700.

css/fontVariant.notes: RN supports fontVariant; Pulp does not.

css/lineHeight.notes: MAJOR GAP -- unitless line-height is the most common form; we drop it.

css/textDecoration.notes: Single-keyword only. Cannot express 'underline dashed red'.

css/textDecorationLine.notes: RN distinguishes textDecoration[Line/Style/Color]; we collapse to one.

css/whiteSpace.notes: Bridge accepts the string but layout enforcement varies.

css/wordBreak.mapsTo: web-compat calls setWordBreak if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/wordWrap.notes: Legacy alias of overflow-wrap. Silent no-op.

css/lineClamp.mapsTo: web-compat calls setLineClamp if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/lineClamp.notes: Silent no-op. Both 'line-clamp' and '-webkit-line-clamp' route here.

css/transform.mapsTo: parseTransform -> setScale | setRotation | setTranslate (and setTransform matrix); transformX/Y separately
css/transform.notes: MAJOR GAP -- only 5 of CSS transform's ~12 functions are honored. RN supports a richer set.

css/transformOrigin.mapsTo: parse keywords + percentages -> setTransformOrigin(id, x, y) (0..1)
css/transformOrigin.notes: Px values are mishandled -- only % and keywords resolve correctly.

css/transition.notes: Pulp ignores the property name and the timing function; only duration is propagated.

css/transitionProperty.notes: All declared transitions animate everything (or nothing depending on backend). Cannot scope.

css/animation.mapsTo: parseTransition + setAnimation -- but setAnimation is a TODO no-op
css/animation.notes: MAJOR GAP -- widget_bridge.cpp:3208 setAnimation is registered but the body is `(void)id; (void)prop;...` -- a stub. CSS @keyframes animations are silently dropped.

css/filter.notes: Bridge parses 'blur(Npx)'. Other filter functions pass through verbatim.

css/mixBlendMode.notes: RN supports mixBlendMode in New Architecture; Pulp does not.

css/touchAction.mapsTo: stored on element instance; not propagated to bridge
css/touchAction.notes: Set but not consumed by the C++ hit-test path.

css/gridTemplateColumns.notes: Forwarded as a string. Renderer honors only what the underlying GridStyle parser accepts. CSS Grid is largely unsupported because Yoga is flex-only.

css/listStyle.notes: Pulp has no list intrinsic; lists are emulated with Col + Label children. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:lists, umbrella issue TBD]

css/listStyleType.notes: See listStyle. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:lists, umbrella issue TBD]

css/listStylePosition.notes: See listStyle. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:lists, umbrella issue TBD]

css/listStyleImage.notes: See listStyle. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:lists, umbrella issue TBD]

css/columns.notes: Multi-column layout out of scope; not aligned with Yoga or RN.

css/scrollBehavior.notes: ScrollView intrinsic handles its own scrolling. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:scroll-augmentation, umbrella issue TBD]

css/scrollSnapType.notes: Not in initial scope. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:scroll-augmentation, umbrella issue TBD]

css/scrollMargin.notes: Not in initial scope. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:scroll-augmentation, umbrella issue TBD]

css/scrollPadding.notes: Not in initial scope. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:scroll-augmentation, umbrella issue TBD]

css/overscrollBehavior.notes: Not in initial scope. [promoted from wontfix-by-omission on 2026-05-03 — see batch batch:scroll-augmentation, umbrella issue TBD]

css/willChange.notes: Hint to compositor -- Pulp's renderer is GPU all the time.

css/direction.notes: RTL writing direction not modeled. Affects logical inset/margin/padding mapping (currently always LTR).

css/verticalAlign.notes: Not relevant in flex-only layout. Could add for Label baseline alignment.

css/printMargin.notes: Print media is out of scope for a GPU plugin renderer.
css/printMargin.batch: wontfix-arch (print media out of scope for GPU plugin renderer)

css/float.notes: CSS float not relevant under a flex-only layout model.

css/__pseudo_classes_note.notes: Pulp routes hover/click/etc. through bridge event registrations (registerHover, registerClick) rather than through CSS pseudo-classes. JSX consumers wire interactivity via on* event props, not via CSS.

rn/alignContent.mapsTo: @pulp/react does not expose alignContent on FlexProps
rn/alignContent.notes: Symmetric with css/alignContent -- also missing in the @pulp/react TS surface (types.ts FlexProps).

rn/alignItems.notes: Pulp's FlexAlign type takes short forms only ({start, center, end, stretch}). RN documentation uses 'flex-start'/'flex-end' which prop-applier passes through verbatim -- these would not match the C++ enum names. Style decl converts 'flex-start'->'start' but @pulp/react does not.

rn/aspectRatio.mapsTo: @pulp/react does not expose aspectRatio. CSS surface attempts to set it via setFlex 'aspect_ratio' but FlexStyle has no field.
rn/aspectRatio.notes: MAJOR -- aspect-ratio is in RN's layout-props surface but not at the @pulp/react JSX surface.

rn/borderBottomWidth.mapsTo: no prop-applier case (border is handled as { color, width, radius } object)
rn/borderBottomWidth.notes: RN prop is flat top-level, Pulp uses object shape. Convert: <View style={{borderBottomWidth: 2}} /> -> <View borderBottom={{color:'#000', width:2}} />. Awkward for RN ports.

rn/borderWidth.mapsTo: Bridge has setBorderWidth, but @pulp/react does not surface it as a flat prop.
rn/borderWidth.notes: User can call <View border={{color:'#000', width:N}} /> as workaround.

rn/borderRadius.mapsTo: Bridge has setBorderRadius, but @pulp/react does not surface it as a flat prop.

rn/borderTopLeftRadius.notes: Bridge has setBorderTopLeftRadius -- needs prop wiring.

rn/borderColor.mapsTo: Bridge has setBorderColor -- not surfaced as flat prop.

rn/display.notes: @pulp/react users opt out via the boolean `visible` prop. No 'contents' equivalent.

rn/flex.mapsTo: no shorthand prop in @pulp/react. Components must use flexGrow/flexShrink/flexBasis individually.
rn/flex.notes: Common RN pattern; awkward to port.

rn/flexDirection.mapsTo: prop-applier 'direction' -> setFlex(id, 'direction', value); but RN says 'row'|'row-reverse'|'column'|'column-reverse'
rn/flexDirection.notes: ALSO MAJOR -- types.ts FlexDirection = 'row' | 'col'. Pulp uses 'col' instead of 'column' (RN convention) -- porting RN code requires renaming. And no reverse modes.

rn/flexWrap.notes: TS type is `boolean` -- RN's enum has 'wrap-reverse' which we cannot express.

rn/justifyContent.notes: Long-form RN names not auto-converted -- same trap as alignItems.

rn/overflow.notes: Plumbing missing in prop-applier; ScrollView intrinsic exposes scroll.

rn/paddingHorizontal.notes: Common RN pattern; awkward to port.

rn/paddingVertical.notes: Common RN pattern; awkward to port.

rn/position.notes: RN's enum is {absolute, relative, static}. Pulp accepts more (fixed, sticky) than RN.

rn/inset.mapsTo: no @pulp/react prop. CSS shim has it.

rn/boxSizing.mapsTo: no branch (and bridge does not register setBoxSizing)

rn/backgroundColor.notes: TS type for `background` is `string` (hex-only by convention).

rn/experimental_backgroundImage.mapsTo: @pulp/react has backgroundGradient; CSS gradient strings only.

rn/backfaceVisibility.mapsTo: Bridge has setBackfaceVisibility -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/elevation.notes: Android-only Material elevation. Pulp uses boxShadow as the cross-platform equivalent.

rn/boxShadow.mapsTo: Bridge has setBoxShadow -- not yet exposed at @pulp/react TS surface.
rn/boxShadow.notes: Could be exposed analogously to `border={...}` shape.

rn/shadowColor.mapsTo: no branch -- would route to setShadow / setBoxShadow
rn/shadowColor.notes: iOS shadow* family -- superseded by boxShadow in modern RN.

rn/filter.mapsTo: Bridge has setFilter -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/pointerEvents.mapsTo: Bridge has setPointerEvents -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/cursor.mapsTo: Bridge has setCursor -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/transform.mapsTo: no @pulp/react prop. CSS surface routes through setScale/setRotation/setTranslate but with reduced function set.
rn/transform.notes: MAJOR -- RN's transform is an array of objects; @pulp/react has none of it.

rn/transformOrigin.mapsTo: Bridge has setTransformOrigin -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/fontFamily.mapsTo: intentionally NOT dispatched in prop-applier.ts (line 152 comment) -- pending #932 SkFontMgr fix
rn/fontFamily.notes: Documented gap in prop-applier; reflects pulp#932 / pulp#927.

rn/fontWeight.notes: Keyword aliasing not done in prop-applier.

rn/textAlignVertical.notes: Android-only in RN; could map to flex align.

rn/textDecorationLine.mapsTo: no branch (use textDecoration in CSS surface only)
rn/textDecorationLine.notes: Use bridge setTextDecoration via CSS surface; @pulp/react does not surface.

rn/textTransform.mapsTo: Bridge has setTextTransform -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

yoga/flexDirection.notes: FlexDirection enum at geometry.hpp:61 has only 2 values. Yoga upstream has 4.

yoga/flexWrap.notes: Bool can only express two states.

yoga/flexBasis.notes: Yoga upstream supports YGAuto / percent; Pulp accepts only px float.

yoga/alignItems.notes: Yoga has YGAlignBaseline; Pulp does not.

yoga/alignContent.mapsTo: no field on FlexStyle and no setFlex case for 'align_content'
yoga/alignContent.notes: MAJOR -- multi-line flex cross-axis distribution unimplementable.

yoga/width.notes: Internal Dimension struct supports vw/vh/% but is not exposed via the setFlex bridge.

yoga/maxWidth.notes: Semantic of 0 differs from CSS unspecified.

yoga/aspectRatio.mapsTo: no FlexStyle field; setFlex 'aspect_ratio' silently dropped
yoga/aspectRatio.notes: Yoga upstream supports it. Pulp's parser exists in CSS shim but the field is missing.

yoga/margin.notes: Yoga has YGAuto for margin centering; not supported.

yoga/borderWidth.mapsTo: no FlexStyle field -- Pulp handles border purely as a paint overlay (View::set_border_*).
yoga/borderWidth.notes: Yoga's BORDER edges contribute to content-box sizing. Pulp's borders are paint-only, not measured by layout. This breaks parity with Yoga measurement when borders are large.

yoga/position.notes: Pulp's enum is a SUPERSET of Yoga's {static, relative, absolute}.

yoga/display.mapsTo: View::set_visible (no Yoga 'display' field -- flex is implicit)
yoga/display.notes: Yoga has YGDisplay {flex, none, contents}; Pulp models only flex+visibility.

yoga/overflow.notes: Yoga has YGOverflow {visible, hidden, scroll}; Pulp omits scroll (use ScrollView intrinsic).

yoga/direction.notes: Yoga supports direction for RTL; Pulp does not.

react._note: Pending second-pass audit. The @pulp/react reconciler surface (host-config.ts, prop-applier.ts) is captured under rn/* above. This section is reserved for React-specific concerns: Suspense, ConcurrentMode, Profiler, error boundaries, refs, portals, hooks edge cases.

html._note: Pending second-pass audit. The DOM-lite consumer surface (createElement, setAttribute, appendChild, addEventListener, etc.) lives in core/view/js/web-compat-*.js. Surfaces of interest: Element / HTMLElement attribute coverage, ARIA attributes, dataset, classList, the DocumentFragment shim, the Event constructor surface.

canvas2d._note: Pending second-pass audit. The Canvas2D shim (web-compat-canvas.js -> bridge canvas* family in widget_bridge.cpp) covers ~50 ops (canvasFillRect, canvasArc, canvasBezier, canvasGradient, canvasPath, canvasMeasureText, canvasGetImageData, canvasPutImageData, etc.). Spec source: HTML Living Standard CanvasRenderingContext2D.

detected-formats.notes: Anthropic Labs Claude Design — manually-exported standalone HTML. Detect via the inline bundler script tag inserted by the Claude Design export. Loader-shell HTML wraps the bundled React app; the static parser sees ~9 elements until the bundle is executed.

Material 3 token vocab. Stitch ZIP / directory exports include code.html, DESIGN.md, screen.png at the top level. Tailwind CDN configured with the forms + container-queries plugins is the strongest single signal.
